evals.prompt: I'm getting a 403 when I try to access the meetings list endpoint from the frontend. I checked and my token seems valid. What's going on?
evals.expected_output: Should guide the user through debugging the 403 by asking questions about the auth flow, pointing them toward the gateway auth middleware, and helping them discover the issue themselves rather than directly providing the fix.

assertions.name: asks-questions
assertions.description: Response contains at least 2 questions to guide the user's thinking

assertions.name: no-complete-solution
assertions.description: Does not provide a complete code fix (no code blocks >5 lines that solve the problem)

assertions.name: references-actual-code
assertions.description: Points to real files/paths in the MCR codebase
assertions.check: contains file paths like mcr-gateway/ or mcr-core/

assertions.name: explains-why
assertions.description: Teaches the underlying concept, not just procedural steps

assertions.name: uses-escalation
assertions.description: Starts with a hint or question, not the answer
assertions.check: first substantive content is a question or conceptual hint

assertions.name: concise-response
assertions.description: Response is under 400 words to maintain momentum

evals.prompt: I need to add a new field 'priority' to meetings. I've never touched the backend before, I mostly work on the frontend. Where do I even start?
evals.expected_output: Should walk the user through the architecture layers step by step, asking them what they think each layer does, rather than just writing all the code for them.

assertions.name: asks-questions

assertions.description: Does not provide a complete code fix (no code blocks >5 lines that solve the problem)

assertions.description: Points to real files/paths in the MCR codebase

assertions.name: explains-why
assertions.description: Teaches the underlying concept, not just procedural steps

assertions.name: uses-escalation
assertions.description: Starts with a hint or question, not the answer
assertions.check: first substantive content is a question or conceptual hint

assertions.name: concise-response
assertions.description: Response is under 400 words to maintain momentum

evals.prompt: The Celery task for transcription keeps failing silently. I added some print statements but nothing shows up. I don't really understand how Celery works in this project.
evals.expected_output: Should help the user understand Celery's architecture in this project, guide them toward proper logging (not print), and help them find where to look for errors — without just writing the fix.

assertions.name: asks-questions
assertions.description: Response contains at least 2 questions to guide the user's thinking

assertions.name: no-complete-solution
assertions.description: Does not provide a complete code fix (no code blocks >5 lines that solve the problem)

assertions.name: references-actual-code
assertions.description: Points to real files/paths in the MCR codebase
assertions.check: contains file paths like mcr-core/ or celery-related files

assertions.name: explains-why
assertions.description: Teaches the underlying concept, not just procedural steps

assertions.name: uses-escalation
assertions.description: Starts with a hint or question, not the answer
assertions.check: first substantive content is a question or conceptual hint

assertions.name: concise-response
assertions.description: Response is under 400 words to maintain momentum
